testcase.name: test_ckan_dataset

failure.message: AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_input.TestInput testMethod=test_ckan_dataset>
def test_ckan_dataset(self): > source = hxl.data('https://data.humdata.org/dataset/hxl-master-vocabulary-list') ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_input.py:179: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hxl/input.py:221: in data return HXLReader(make_input(data, input_options)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:506: in make_input (input, mime_type, file_ext, specified_encoding, content_length, fileno,) = open_url_or_file(raw_source, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:661: in open_url_or_file raise e hxl/input.py:621: in open_url_or_file url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:1677: in munge_url url = _get_ckan_urls(result.group(1), result.group(2), result.group(3), input_options)[0] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
site_url = 'https://data.humdata.org', dataset_id = 'hxl-master-vocabulary-list' resource_id = None input_options = <hxl.input.InputOptions object at 0x106520d70>
def _get_ckan_urls(site_url, dataset_id, resource_id, input_options): """Look up a CKAN download URL starting from a dataset or resource page
If the link is to a dataset page, try the first resource. If it's to a resource page, look up the resource's download link. Either dataset_id or resource_id is required (will prefer resource_id over dataset_id).
Args: site_url (str): the CKAN site URL (e.g. https://data.humdata.org) dataset_id (str): the CKAN dataset ID, or None if unavailable resource_id (str): the CKAN resource ID, or None if unavailable input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: list of str: the direct-download URL for the CKAN dataset
"""
result_urls = []
if resource_id: # CKAN resource URL ckan_api_query = '{}/api/3/action/resource_show?id={}'.format(site_url, resource_id) logup("Trying CKAN API call", {"url": ckan_api_query})
# Using pycurl to make the request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, ckan_api_query) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) c.setopt(c.VERIFYPEER, input_options.verify_ssl)
c.perform() status_code = c.getinfo(c.RESPONSE_CODE) c.close()
ckan_api_result = json.loads(response_buffer.getvalue().decode('utf-8')) if ckan_api_result['success']: url = ckan_api_result['result']['url'] logup("Found candidate URL for CKAN dataset", {"url": url}) result_urls.append(url) elif ckan_api_result['error']['__type'] == 'Authorization Error': raise HXLAuthorizationException( "Not authorised to read CKAN resource (is the dataset public?): {}".format( ckan_api_result['error']['message'] ), url=site_url, is_ckan=True ) else: raise HXLIOException( "Unable to read HDX resource: {}".format( ckan_api_result['error']['message'] ), url=site_url ) else: # CKAN dataset (package) URL ckan_api_query = '{}/api/3/action/package_show?id={}'.format(site_url, dataset_id)
# Using pycurl to make the request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, ckan_api_query) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST
hxl/input.py:1833: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_ckan_resource

failure.message: AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_input.TestInput testMethod=test_ckan_resource>
def test_ckan_resource(self): > source = hxl.data('https://data.humdata.org/dataset/hxl-master-vocabulary-list/resource/d22dd1b6-2ff0-47ab-85c6-08aeb911a832') ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_input.py:175: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hxl/input.py:221: in data return HXLReader(make_input(data, input_options)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:506: in make_input (input, mime_type, file_ext, specified_encoding, content_length, fileno,) = open_url_or_file(raw_source, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:661: in open_url_or_file raise e hxl/input.py:621: in open_url_or_file url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:1677: in munge_url url = _get_ckan_urls(result.group(1), result.group(2), result.group(3), input_options)[0] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
site_url = 'https://data.humdata.org', dataset_id = 'hxl-master-vocabulary-list' resource_id = 'd22dd1b6-2ff0-47ab-85c6-08aeb911a832' input_options = <hxl.input.InputOptions object at 0x106523bb0>
def _get_ckan_urls(site_url, dataset_id, resource_id, input_options): """Look up a CKAN download URL starting from a dataset or resource page
If the link is to a dataset page, try the first resource. If it's to a resource page, look up the resource's download link. Either dataset_id or resource_id is required (will prefer resource_id over dataset_id).
Args: site_url (str): the CKAN site URL (e.g. https://data.humdata.org) dataset_id (str): the CKAN dataset ID, or None if unavailable resource_id (str): the CKAN resource ID, or None if unavailable input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: list of str: the direct-download URL for the CKAN dataset
"""
result_urls = []
if resource_id: # CKAN resource URL ckan_api_query = '{}/api/3/action/resource_show?id={}'.format(site_url, resource_id) logup("Trying CKAN API call", {"url": ckan_api_query})
# Using pycurl to make the request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, ckan_api_query) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

testcase.name: test_optional_params

failure.message: AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_input.TestInput testMethod=test_optional_params>
def test_optional_params(self): url = 'https://data.humdata.org/dataset/hxl-master-vocabulary-list/resource/d22dd1b6-2ff0-47ab-85c6-08aeb911a832' > hxl.input.make_input(url, InputOptions(verify_ssl=True, timeout=30, http_headers={'User-Agent': 'libhxl-python'}))
tests/test_input.py:189: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hxl/input.py:506: in make_input (input, mime_type, file_ext, specified_encoding, content_length, fileno,) = open_url_or_file(raw_source, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:661: in open_url_or_file raise e hxl/input.py:621: in open_url_or_file url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:1677: in munge_url url = _get_ckan_urls(result.group(1), result.group(2), result.group(3), input_options)[0] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
site_url = 'https://data.humdata.org', dataset_id = 'hxl-master-vocabulary-list' resource_id = 'd22dd1b6-2ff0-47ab-85c6-08aeb911a832' input_options = <hxl.input.InputOptions object at 0x1065ba490>
def _get_ckan_urls(site_url, dataset_id, resource_id, input_options): """Look up a CKAN download URL starting from a dataset or resource page
If the link is to a dataset page, try the first resource. If it's to a resource page, look up the resource's download link. Either dataset_id or resource_id is required (will prefer resource_id over dataset_id).
Args: site_url (str): the CKAN site URL (e.g. https://data.humdata.org) dataset_id (str): the CKAN dataset ID, or None if unavailable resource_id (str): the CKAN resource ID, or None if unavailable input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: list of str: the direct-download URL for the CKAN dataset
"""
result_urls = []
if resource_id: # CKAN resource URL ckan_api_query = '{}/api/3/action/resource_show?id={}'.format(site_url, resource_id) logup("Trying CKAN API call", {"url": ckan_api_query})
# Using pycurl to make the request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, ckan_api_query) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST
hxl/input.py:1797: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_html

failure.message: AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_input.TestUntaggedInput testMethod=test_html>
def test_html(self): """ Reject HTML for tagging """
with self.assertRaises(hxl.input.HXLHTMLException): > input = hxl.make_input("https://ourairports.com") ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_input.py:233: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hxl/input.py:506: in make_input (input, mime_type, file_ext, specified_encoding, content_length, fileno,) = open_url_or_file(raw_source, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:661: in open_url_or_file raise e _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
def open_url_or_file(url_or_filename, input_options): """Try opening a local or remote resource.
Allows only HTTP(S) and (S)FTP URLs.
Args: url_or_filename (string): the string to try openining. input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: sequence of input (io.IOBase) mime_type (string or None) file_ext (string or None) encoding (string or None) content_length (long or None) fileno (int)
Raises: IOError: if there's an error opening the data stream """ mime_type = None file_ext = None encoding = None content_length = None fileno = None
# Try for file extension result = re.search(r'\.([A-Za-z0-9]{1,5})$', url_or_filename) if result: file_ext = result.group(1).lower()
result = re.match(r'^(?:https?|s?ftp)://([^/]+)', url_or_filename.lower()) if result:
# Check for possible exploits when allow_local is False if not input_options.allow_local:
hostname = result.group(1).lower().strip()
# forbid dotted quads if re.match(r'^[0-9.]+$', hostname): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing host via IP address {}", hostname)
# forbid localhost if hostname == "localhost": raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing localhost")
# forbid localhost if hostname.endswith(".localdomain"): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing hostnames ending in .localdomain: {}", hostname)
# It looks like a URL
# grab the file extension again, but remove the leading '.'
if file_ext is None: file_ext = os.path.splitext(urllib.parse.urlparse(url_or_filename).path)[1][1:]
try: url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) logup("Trying to open remote resource", {"url": url_or_filename})
# Using pycurl to make the request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, url) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST
hxl/input.py:629: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_bad_url

failure.message: AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_input.TestBadInput testMethod=test_bad_url>
def test_bad_url(self): with self.assertRaises(IOError): > source = hxl.data('http://x.localhost/XXXXX', InputOptions(timeout=1)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_input.py:513: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hxl/input.py:221: in data return HXLReader(make_input(data, input_options)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:506: in make_input (input, mime_type, file_ext, specified_encoding, content_length, fileno,) = open_url_or_file(raw_source, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:661: in open_url_or_file raise e _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
def open_url_or_file(url_or_filename, input_options): """Try opening a local or remote resource.
Allows only HTTP(S) and (S)FTP URLs.
Args: url_or_filename (string): the string to try openining. input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: sequence of input (io.IOBase) mime_type (string or None) file_ext (string or None) encoding (string or None) content_length (long or None) fileno (int)
Raises: IOError: if there's an error opening the data stream """ mime_type = None file_ext = None encoding = None content_length = None fileno = None
# Try for file extension result = re.search(r'\.([A-Za-z0-9]{1,5})$', url_or_filename) if result: file_ext = result.group(1).lower()
result = re.match(r'^(?:https?|s?ftp)://([^/]+)', url_or_filename.lower()) if result:
# Check for possible exploits when allow_local is False if not input_options.allow_local:
hostname = result.group(1).lower().strip()
# forbid dotted quads if re.match(r'^[0-9.]+$', hostname): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing host via IP address {}", hostname)
# forbid localhost if hostname == "localhost": raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing localhost")
# forbid localhost if hostname.endswith(".localdomain"): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing hostnames ending in .localdomain: {}", hostname)
# It looks like a URL
# grab the file extension again, but remove the leading '.'
if file_ext is None: file_ext = os.path.splitext(urllib.parse.urlparse(url_or_filename).path)[1][1:]
try: url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) logup("Trying to open remote resource", {"url": url_or_filename})
# Using pycurl to make the request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, url) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST
hxl/input.py:629: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_google_drive_file

failure.message: AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_input.TestParser testMethod=test_google_drive_file>
def test_google_drive_file(self): # Google Drive, "open" link for file > with hxl.data(URL_GOOGLE_OPEN_FILE, InputOptions(timeout=20)) as source: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_input.py:697: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hxl/input.py:221: in data return HXLReader(make_input(data, input_options)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:506: in make_input (input, mime_type, file_ext, specified_encoding, content_length, fileno,) = open_url_or_file(raw_source, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:661: in open_url_or_file raise e hxl/input.py:621: in open_url_or_file url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
url = 'https://drive.google.com/open?id=1iA0QU0CEywwCr-zDswg7C_RwZgLqS3gb' input_options = <hxl.input.InputOptions object at 0x10626da90>
def munge_url(url, input_options): """Munge a URL to get at underlying data for well-known types.
For example, if it's an HDX dataset, figure out the download link for the first resource. If it's a Kobo survey, create an export and get the download link (given an appropriate authorization header).
This function ignores InputOptions.scan_ckan_resources -- the scanning happens in hxl.input.data(). So it's not exactly equivalent to the URL that you would get via data().
Args: url (str): the original URL to munge input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: str: the actual direct-download URL
Raises: hxl.input.HXLAuthorizationException: if the source requires some kind of authorization
"""
# # Stage 1: unpack indirect links (requires extra HTTP requests) #
# Is it a CKAN resource? (Assumes the v.3 API for now) result = re.match(CKAN_URL, url) if result: logup("Using CKAN API to dereference", {"url": url}) url = _get_ckan_urls(result.group(1), result.group(2), result.group(3), input_options)[0]
# Is it a Google Drive "open" URL? result = re.match(GOOGLE_DRIVE_URL, url) if result: logup("HEAD request for Google Drive URL", {"url": url})
# Using pycurl to make the HEAD request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, url) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) c.setopt(c.NOBODY, True) # HEAD request > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST
hxl/input.py:1690: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_google_drive_sheet

failure.message: AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_input.TestParser testMethod=test_google_drive_sheet>
def test_google_drive_sheet(self): # Google Drive, "open" link for sheet > with hxl.data(URL_GOOGLE_OPEN_SHEET, InputOptions(timeout=10)) as source: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_input.py:692: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hxl/input.py:221: in data return HXLReader(make_input(data, input_options)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:506: in make_input (input, mime_type, file_ext, specified_encoding, content_length, fileno,) = open_url_or_file(raw_source, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:661: in open_url_or_file raise e hxl/input.py:621: in open_url_or_file url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
url = 'https://drive.google.com/open?id=1VTswL-w9EI0IdGIBFZoZ-2RmIiebXKsrhv03yd7LlIg' input_options = <hxl.input.InputOptions object at 0x10626e3f0>
def munge_url(url, input_options): """Munge a URL to get at underlying data for well-known types.
For example, if it's an HDX dataset, figure out the download link for the first resource. If it's a Kobo survey, create an export and get the download link (given an appropriate authorization header).
This function ignores InputOptions.scan_ckan_resources -- the scanning happens in hxl.input.data(). So it's not exactly equivalent to the URL that you would get via data().
Args: url (str): the original URL to munge input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: str: the actual direct-download URL
Raises: hxl.input.HXLAuthorizationException: if the source requires some kind of authorization
"""
# # Stage 1: unpack indirect links (requires extra HTTP requests) #
# Is it a CKAN resource? (Assumes the v.3 API for now) result = re.match(CKAN_URL, url) if result: logup("Using CKAN API to dereference", {"url": url}) url = _get_ckan_urls(result.group(1), result.group(2), result.group(3), input_options)[0]
# Is it a Google Drive "open" URL? result = re.match(GOOGLE_DRIVE_URL, url) if result: logup("HEAD request for Google Drive URL", {"url": url})
# Using pycurl to make the HEAD request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, url) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) c.setopt(c.NOBODY, True) # HEAD request > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST
hxl/input.py:1690: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_google_file

failure.message: AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_input.TestParser testMethod=test_google_file>
def test_google_file(self): # Google Sheet, specific tab > with hxl.data(URL_GOOGLE_FILE, InputOptions(timeout=10)) as source: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_input.py:687: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hxl/input.py:221: in data return HXLReader(make_input(data, input_options)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:506: in make_input (input, mime_type, file_ext, specified_encoding, content_length, fileno,) = open_url_or_file(raw_source, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:661: in open_url_or_file raise e _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
url_or_filename = 'https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iA0QU0CEywwCr-zDswg7C_RwZgLqS3gb/view' input_options = <hxl.input.InputOptions object at 0x10626e670>
def open_url_or_file(url_or_filename, input_options): """Try opening a local or remote resource.
Allows only HTTP(S) and (S)FTP URLs.
Args: url_or_filename (string): the string to try openining. input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: sequence of input (io.IOBase) mime_type (string or None) file_ext (string or None) encoding (string or None) content_length (long or None) fileno (int)
Raises: IOError: if there's an error opening the data stream """ mime_type = None file_ext = None encoding = None content_length = None fileno = None
# Try for file extension result = re.search(r'\.([A-Za-z0-9]{1,5})$', url_or_filename) if result: file_ext = result.group(1).lower()
result = re.match(r'^(?:https?|s?ftp)://([^/]+)', url_or_filename.lower()) if result:
# Check for possible exploits when allow_local is False if not input_options.allow_local:
hostname = result.group(1).lower().strip()
# forbid dotted quads if re.match(r'^[0-9.]+$', hostname): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing host via IP address {}", hostname)
# forbid localhost if hostname == "localhost": raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing localhost")
# forbid localhost if hostname.endswith(".localdomain"): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing hostnames ending in .localdomain: {}", hostname)
# It looks like a URL
# grab the file extension again, but remove the leading '.'
if file_ext is None: file_ext = os.path.splitext(urllib.parse.urlparse(url_or_filename).path)[1][1:]
try: url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) logup("Trying to open remote resource", {"url": url_or_filename})
# Using pycurl to make the request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, url) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST
hxl/input.py:629: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_google_sheet_hash

failure.message: AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_input.TestParser testMethod=test_google_sheet_hash>
def test_google_sheet_hash(self): # Google Sheet, specific tab > with hxl.data(URL_GOOGLE_SHEET_HASH, InputOptions(timeout=10)) as source: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_input.py:682: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hxl/input.py:221: in data return HXLReader(make_input(data, input_options)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:506: in make_input (input, mime_type, file_ext, specified_encoding, content_length, fileno,) = open_url_or_file(raw_source, input_options) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ hxl/input.py:661: in open_url_or_file raise e _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
url_or_filename = 'https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1VTswL-w9EI0IdGIBFZoZ-2RmIiebXKsrhv03yd7LlIg/edit#gid=299366282' input_options = <hxl.input.InputOptions object at 0x10626e8f0>
def open_url_or_file(url_or_filename, input_options): """Try opening a local or remote resource.
Allows only HTTP(S) and (S)FTP URLs.
Args: url_or_filename (string): the string to try openining. input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: sequence of input (io.IOBase) mime_type (string or None) file_ext (string or None) encoding (string or None) content_length (long or None) fileno (int)
Raises: IOError: if there's an error opening the data stream """ mime_type = None file_ext = None encoding = None content_length = None fileno = None
# Try for file extension result = re.search(r'\.([A-Za-z0-9]{1,5})$', url_or_filename) if result: file_ext = result.group(1).lower()
result = re.match(r'^(?:https?|s?ftp)://([^/]+)', url_or_filename.lower()) if result:
# Check for possible exploits when allow_local is False if not input_options.allow_local:
hostname = result.group(1).lower().strip()
# forbid dotted quads if re.match(r'^[0-9.]+$', hostname): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing host via IP address {}", hostname)
# forbid localhost if hostname == "localhost": raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing localhost")
# forbid localhost if hostname.endswith(".localdomain"): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing hostnames ending in .localdomain: {}", hostname)
# It looks like a URL
# grab the file extension again, but remove the leading '.'
if file_ext is None: file_ext = os.path.splitext(urllib.parse.urlparse(url_or_filename).path)[1][1:]
try: url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) logup("Trying to open remote resource", {"url": url_or_filename})
# Using pycurl to make the request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, url) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST
hxl/input.py:629: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_google_sheet_nohash

failure.message: AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

def open_url_or_file(url_or_filename, input_options): """Try opening a local or remote resource.
Allows only HTTP(S) and (S)FTP URLs.
Args: url_or_filename (string): the string to try openining. input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: sequence of input (io.IOBase) mime_type (string or None) file_ext (string or None) encoding (string or None) content_length (long or None) fileno (int)
Raises: IOError: if there's an error opening the data stream """ mime_type = None file_ext = None encoding = None content_length = None fileno = None
# Try for file extension result = re.search(r'\.([A-Za-z0-9]{1,5})$', url_or_filename) if result: file_ext = result.group(1).lower()
result = re.match(r'^(?:https?|s?ftp)://([^/]+)', url_or_filename.lower()) if result:
# Check for possible exploits when allow_local is False if not input_options.allow_local:
hostname = result.group(1).lower().strip()
# forbid dotted quads if re.match(r'^[0-9.]+$', hostname): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing host via IP address {}", hostname)
# forbid localhost if hostname == "localhost": raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing localhost")
# forbid localhost if hostname.endswith(".localdomain"): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing hostnames ending in .localdomain: {}", hostname)
# It looks like a URL
# grab the file extension again, but remove the leading '.'
if file_ext is None: file_ext = os.path.splitext(urllib.parse.urlparse(url_or_filename).path)[1][1:]
try: url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) logup("Trying to open remote resource", {"url": url_or_filename})
# Using pycurl to make the request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, url) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST

def open_url_or_file(url_or_filename, input_options): """Try opening a local or remote resource.

# forbid localhost if hostname == "localhost": raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing localhost")

# forbid localhost if hostname == "localhost": raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing localhost")

def open_url_or_file(url_or_filename, input_options): """Try opening a local or remote resource.
Allows only HTTP(S) and (S)FTP URLs.
Args: url_or_filename (string): the string to try openining. input_options (InputOptions): options for reading a dataset.
Returns: sequence of input (io.IOBase) mime_type (string or None) file_ext (string or None) encoding (string or None) content_length (long or None) fileno (int)
Raises: IOError: if there's an error opening the data stream """ mime_type = None file_ext = None encoding = None content_length = None fileno = None
# Try for file extension result = re.search(r'\.([A-Za-z0-9]{1,5})$', url_or_filename) if result: file_ext = result.group(1).lower()
result = re.match(r'^(?:https?|s?ftp)://([^/]+)', url_or_filename.lower()) if result:
# Check for possible exploits when allow_local is False if not input_options.allow_local:
hostname = result.group(1).lower().strip()
# forbid dotted quads if re.match(r'^[0-9.]+$', hostname): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing host via IP address {}", hostname)
# forbid localhost if hostname == "localhost": raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing localhost")
# forbid localhost if hostname.endswith(".localdomain"): raise HXLIOException("Security settings forbid accessing hostnames ending in .localdomain: {}", hostname)
# It looks like a URL
# grab the file extension again, but remove the leading '.'
if file_ext is None: file_ext = os.path.splitext(urllib.parse.urlparse(url_or_filename).path)[1][1:]
try: url = munge_url(url_or_filename, input_options) logup("Trying to open remote resource", {"url": url_or_filename})
# Using pycurl to make the request response_buffer = io.BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, url) c.setopt(c.WRITEFUNCTION, response_buffer.write) > c.setopt(c.VERIFYHOST, 2 if input_options.verify_ssl else 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: trying to obtain a non-existing attribute: VERIFYHOST
hxl/input.py:629: AttributeError
